verdict.label: Probably skip
verdict.headline: No reliable window
verdict.reason: Main blocker: Surfline consistency is lully.

conversationalSummary.text: TODAY looks like a probably-skip day. Even though the wind fit is decent, Surfline is poor-to-fair and the waves are coming in way too soft, so Cerro at lunch is likely to be smaller than your fun size. I’d check Surfline and the MEO Beachcam first, and unless they show a noticeably better pattern than expected, it’s probably not worth the drive.

best.windFit: WSW 15 km/h · cross-off · good wind fit
best.bestWind: ideal SSW, usable S/SW/SSE/SE, clean <=22 km/h

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Cerro looks like a clean small window for Too soft, Medium confidence: 0-0.3 m, energy not captured, 9 s period, lully 0/100, and good wind at 15 km/h.  A sheltered beachbreak with gentle lefts and rights, somewhat similar to Cantinho but less consistent; this is best for manageable bodyboard waves when north-northwest swell reaches the bay with clean southerly wind; 6-9s at low tide can still make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia punchy; worth keeping on the radar because Surfline is showing it above Poor while most other local spots are Poor..  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; Cerro looks below your bodyboard fun size.
best.shortExplanation: Cerro is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
warnings: Cerro looks below your bodyboard fun size
